---
title: "LRPGF02: Factors associated with gains in receptive vocabulary (R)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF02 is a **gain-factors** model for receptive vocabulary (`rowpvt`): what is associated with how much children gain across the three period transitions (ANCOVA - the period post-count given its pre-count - Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept). It conditions on the own baseline, age, cognitive ability (block design), the upstream DAG skill taught receptive vocabulary (TR), and the revised-DAG confounders hearing (HS = `hs`) and phonological memory (RW = `erbto`) — each entered with a missing-indicator — with focal interactions group$\times$ability, group$\times$own-baseline, age$\times$ability.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

**Only the randomised on-intervention term is causal.** Under the revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised 2026-07-10) the **period-1 average marginal effect** — the genuinely randomised, all-untreated-baseline transition — is the ITT-anchor estimand; `beta_trt` itself is only the on-intervention log-odds contrast, not the ITT effect. Every other coefficient is an **adjusted association**: the child random intercept is a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity and does **not** control latent general ability, so these internal slopes stay **descriptive associations** - report as "associated with", never "drives". Intervention sessions/dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded (not a DAG node; statistically redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the period post-count, $N = 170$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \beta_{\text{trt}}\text{OnIntervention} + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(\text{pre}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{s\in\{TR\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}}) + \sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c) + \sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}$, where the $z(c)$ are the standardised revised-DAG confounders hearing (HS = `hs`) and phonological memory (RW = `erbto`), each with a missing-indicator. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
